model.knowledgebaseCapability: every Knowledgebase is Wiki-capable without a special Knowledgebase type or conversion
model.publicationCardinality: exactly one canonical WikiPublication per tenant and Knowledgebase Space
model.initialPublicationState: DRAFT with REVIEW_REQUIRED mode and PRIVATE default visibility
model.providerReuse: one canonical WikiPublication may be referenced by multiple authorized Deploy Site Resources, Sites, Variants and Mounts
model.siteRevisionRule: Only Deploy-owned Site, Resource, Variant, Mount, Binding, delivery, security or observability configuration changes compile a new SiteRevision. Wiki content, visibility, publication mode, theme, renderer, navigation and search-generation changes remain provider lifecycle state and advance provider events or generations instead.

mutationModel.logicalFileIdentity: driveNodeUuid remains stable across ordinary content updates, rename and same-root move
mutationModel.contentUpdate: editing or replacing a logical file commits a new immutable driveVersionUuid and atomically advances the node current version
mutationModel.physicalImmutability: a committed Drive version, checksum and backing blob are immutable
mutationModel.renameOrMove: the stable node receives a new normalized source path observation and route reconciliation invalidates old and new routes
mutationModel.deleteOrRestore: delete and restore are version-fenced lifecycle transitions on the stable node
mutationModel.forbidden: rejecting a new version merely because the logical sources/raw path already exists

upload.completionRule: Drive completion creates or updates an authenticated source projection; it never grants anonymous visibility.

formatSupport.classificationAuthority: normalized extension, detected MIME, signature sniffing and scan result must agree before a processor is selected

native_page.behavior: parse with a versioned bounded text processor and render as a sanitized Wiki page

safe_html_page.behavior: parse into a DOM, remove active content, rewrite eligible local links and assets, sanitize to the approved HTML profile and render on the Wiki origin

document_page.behavior: provide a bounded document viewer, extracted text and thumbnails; office formats use an isolated converter to create approved PDF or page-image renditions

presentation_page.behavior: use an isolated converter to produce slide images or PDF, extract slide text and notes, and expose slide-aware navigation

spreadsheet_page.behavior: show a bounded read-only sheet/table preview, expose sheet navigation and index bounded cell text; formulas are never executed by the Wiki runtime

source_code_page.behavior: render escaped syntax-highlighted source with line anchors, copy and download controls, and bounded text indexing

media_page.behavior: use an approved image, audio or video viewer with metadata, poster or thumbnail renditions and range delivery when supported

archive_download.behavior: scan and expose a bounded safe manifest when supported; never execute or render archive members implicitly; original download requires attachment disposition

unsupported_binary.behavior: never render or execute; quarantine or offer an explicitly approved attachment-only workflow

formatSupport.mdxRule: MDX is treated as Markdown text with JSX disabled unless a separately governed trusted component profile is approved.
formatSupport.javascriptRule: Uploaded JavaScript is supported as source content and download material, but never executes on the standard Wiki origin.
formatSupport.activeSiteRule: Executable JavaScript, active HTML, service workers, WebAssembly and arbitrary CSS require a separate isolated-origin trusted-active-site profile with signed packages, supply-chain evidence and explicit security approval.
formatSupport.mismatchRule: extension, declared MIME and detected signature mismatch blocks public readiness and creates a bounded author problem.
formatSupport.securityMetadataRule: publication state, visibility, permissions, scan state and execution policy are never elevated by source-embedded metadata.

publication.provisioning: idempotently create with every new Knowledgebase and bounded-backfill existing eligible Knowledgebases before feature activation
publication.authorWorkspaceRule: Drive completion makes the source row immediately visible to authorized authors; processing progress and private preview update without a public publication command.
publication.publicWikiRule: The standard default requires an explicit version-fenced publish command after all gates pass. Upload completion and preview readiness never publish by themselves.
publication.autoPublicRule: AUTO_PUBLIC_AFTER_CHECKS is an owner-approved opt-in policy scoped to a site or explicit import rule; it runs the same gates and publish command with an attributed system actor.
publication.scheduledRule: SCHEDULED content is revalidated at execution time and publishes only the pinned ready source version.
publication.bulkRule: Bulk upload remains private; authorized users may review the ready subset and issue one bounded bulk publish command with per-item outcomes.

sourceProjection.unlistedRule: Resolvable by canonical URL but excluded from navigation, sitemap and search.

renditions.authority: derived and rebuildable presentation state; never the source-content authority
renditions.storage: Drive-backed generated artifact written through sdkwork-drive-uploader-service or an approved server-side facade
renditions.reuse: content-addressed renditions may be reused only within authorized tenant and policy scope
renditions.cleanup: superseded renditions follow bounded retention and Drive-owned cleanup; current eligible source versions remain protected
forbidden: converter output trusted without scan and checksum validation

processorRegistry.selection: detected format plus configured policy resolves exactly one processor profile
processorRegistry.execution: document and presentation conversion runs in an isolated worker or approved converter service with no ambient credentials, no private-network access, read-only input and bounded temporary storage
processorRegistry.failure: processor crash, timeout, malformed output, decompression bomb, macro or active-content detection fails closed and preserves the last verified public version only when policy allows
processorRegistry.upgrade: processor-version changes enqueue bounded reprocessing and support canary, compatibility evidence and rollback

projectionPipeline.driveEventAuthority: a Drive-owned AsyncAPI contract under sdkwork-drive/apis/events must exist before implementation readiness
projectionPipeline.ordering: newer Drive version and explicit publication version fence older work
steps: validate event, tenant, Drive binding and sources/raw ancestry
upsert source projection by stable Drive identity
read bounded metadata and bytes through a typed Drive port
apply scan, size and MIME policy
classify format from extension, MIME, signature and scan state
parse native pages or select an approved isolated processor
generate and verify required renditions and extracted text
derive canonical route, locale, headings, links, assets, document outline and navigation metadata
validate renderer, converter and sanitizer output
commit projection and public-version state with an outbox event
projectionPipeline.retry: bounded exponential retry with jitter, dead letter and operator replay
projectionPipeline.reconciliation: checkpointed bounded Drive scan repairs event gaps without losing explicit publication decisions

routing.entrypoint: standalone and cloud requests both enter sdkwork-web-server through an active sdkwork.website-runtime.v1 descriptor; Knowledgebase exposes no anonymous fixed public route
routing.baseUrl: an active Binding selects a Variant whose longest-prefix WIKI Mount references a valid KNOWLEDGEBASE_WIKI Site Resource
routing.standaloneComposition: Web Server may inject the typed Knowledgebase provider in-process but preserves the same descriptor, routing, authorization, error and cache contracts
routing.mountTranslation: Deploy ROOT or ALIAS translation produces a normalized provider-relative Wiki route that cannot ascend above the publication root.
routing.homepage: sources/raw/index.md unless an explicit eligible homepage is configured
routing.pageMapping: remove one processor-declared page extension and use a canonical trailing slash
routing.documentExample: sources/raw/manual.pdf maps to /manual/ for the Wiki wrapper; original bytes are available only through a policy-checked pinned-version download representation
routing.directoryIndex: index.md maps to its directory route
routing.routeCollision: different source files that normalize to the same canonical route block public readiness until an explicit route override resolves the conflict
routing.redirects: reviewed move and rename redirects only; no cycles, chains beyond policy, or open redirects

provider.transport: sdkwork-knowledgebase-internal-sdk in split topology or the equivalent typed Rust service port in standalone topology
forbiddenTransport: database reads from Deploy or Web Server
provider.connectionCardinality: many Deploy Site Resources may reference one providerResourceUuid; Knowledgebase stores no singular Site, Mount, domain or certificate authority

descriptorReference.resourceUuid: Deploy-owned deploy_site_resource UUID used by routing, cache and observations
descriptorReference.providerResourceUuid: Knowledgebase WikiPublication UUID used by the provider as aggregate identity
descriptorReference.providerRootNodeUuid: last validated sources/raw root UUID; diagnostic and confinement evidence, not provider identity

operations.openContent: openWikiAsset or openWikiRendition for an exact pinned public representation
operations.searchWiki: searchWiki(providerResourceUuid, query, cursor, pageSize, locale, runtimeContext)
wikiExtensions: open pinned original download when policy permits

webServerOwns: host, Binding, Variant and Mount routing
platformAlignment.deploymentRule: Provider lifecycle changes never create Deploy Release, Deployment or SiteRevision; they advance the narrowest applicable public version or generation and emit idempotent provider events.
platformAlignment.tlsRule: TLS snapshots advance independently from WebsiteRuntimeDescriptor and Wiki content versions.

rendering.profile: versioned multi-format renderer registry with CommonMark-compatible, safe HTML, document, presentation, spreadsheet, source-code and media profiles
rendering.externalFetch: disabled or delegated to an approved SSRF-safe capability

assets.source: an eligible ASSET projection under sources/raw
assets.publicRule: A public page reference never upgrades a private or unready asset.
assets.openRule: resolve the pinned verified Drive version through a typed Drive stream port

searchAndNavigation.searchSource: only current eligible public versions and their verified bounded text renditions
formatExtraction: spreadsheet sheet names and bounded cell text
forbidden: client slicing of a full result set
RAG index as public publication authority

generations.driveSourceCheckpoint: durable ordered consumer checkpoint for the bound sources/raw Drive change stream; never part of a public cache key
generations.providerGeneration: monotonic WikiPublication-wide fencing value changed only by provider eligibility, fixed-root binding, renderer/theme policy or provider-wide route-policy transitions
generations.pagePublicVersion: monotonic per-route public representation version changed by publish, republish, move, unpublish, delete, quarantine or visibility revocation
generations.searchGeneration: monotonic public search index snapshot version
generations.privateProcessingRule: upload progress, private preview, parse retries and private index work do not advance providerGeneration or a page public version
generations.ordinaryPageUpdateRule: an ordinary page-body update advances only its pagePublicVersion plus affected navigation or search generations; it does not flush every route
generations.revocationRule: revocation commits the new pagePublicVersion and revocation event in one transaction before stale delivery can continue

freshness.nativeAutoPublishApplicability: supported native page or preverified asset within the approved small-file class while Drive, scan, processor, event and Web Server dependencies are healthy
freshness.realTimeDefinition: event-driven bounded eventual visibility without polling-only publication, Deploy Release, Deployment or SiteRevision
freshness.measurementStart: the committed explicit, automatic, or scheduled publish transition that changes the eligible public version; never raw upload completion or private preview readiness
freshness.mechanism: versioned projection events plus Web Server cache invalidation or revalidation
freshness.readThrough: event uncertainty or gap forces provider validation against the current public version
freshness.staleRule: last verified public version is allowed only by KEEP_LAST_PUBLIC_UNTIL_READY and never for a security or revocation transition

formatFreshness.nativeAndPreverifiedAsset: uses the base p95 and p99 freshness objective
formatFreshness.conversionRequired: asynchronous job with progress; target is declared per processor profile and file-size class
formatFreshness.largeOrComplexFile: no synchronous freshness claim; current verified public version follows update policy until conversion completes
formatFreshness.revocation: privacy, quarantine, delete and unpublish are not delayed by conversion backlog

cache.eventRule: events reduce freshness latency but do not replace read-through authorization and version validation
cache.failureRule: uncertain generation revalidates; private, delete, quarantine and unpublish cannot serve stale
cache.staleIsolationRule: A last-known-good descriptor or representation may be served only within its exact tenant, Site, Binding, Variant, Mount, resource, provider generation and public-version scope.

events.payloadRule: event id/type/version/source/time/tenant plus providerResourceUuid, providerGeneration, affected normalized routes, old and new pagePublicVersion where applicable, operation, reason code and provider checkpoint only; no bodies, raw source paths, object keys or signed URLs
events.transactionRule: public state/version/generation and the corresponding outbox row commit atomically
events.hotPathRule: Knowledgebase emits provider events directly to Web Server consumers; Deploy is not in the ordinary content update hot path
events.consumerRule: idempotent with explicit retry, dead letter, replay and ordering fences
events.moveRule: A route move invalidates both old and new normalized routes.
events.gapRule: Sequence gap, incompatible event version, checkpoint loss or excessive lag marks the Web Server provider generation uncertain, disables freshness-only cache trust, reconciles through provider metadata, then resumes from a durable checkpoint.
events.correctnessRule: Events improve freshness; provider read-through validation remains the correctness backstop.

securityAndPrivacy.managementAuth: verified SDKWork application context and permissioned optimistic commands
securityAndPrivacy.providerAuth: authenticated service identity with tenant and publication scope
securityAndPrivacy.publicErrors: indistinguishable not found for nonexistent, private, wrong-tenant, paused and unready resources
securityAndPrivacy.retention: Drive source versions, projections, indexes, caches, events and publication decisions participate in export, deletion, residency, legal hold and takedown policy
securityAndPrivacy.metricsRule: no raw path, title, query, host, content or tenant-specific unbounded label

failureAndRecovery.parseRenderOrConversionFailure: mark the new source or rendition version ERROR and apply the configured last-public policy
failureAndRecovery.quarantineOrRevocation: immediate public ineligibility and cache invalidation
failureAndRecovery.searchFailure: page delivery follows the declared search-readiness policy and reports degraded state
failureAndRecovery.providerOrDriveFailure: bounded timeout and circuit; only a previously verified representation allowed by policy may be served
failureAndRecovery.rebuild: recreate projections and indexes from Drive while reattaching retained publication decisions by stable node identity

required: Drive-only resumable bulk upload and stable identity
per-file state transition permissions and optimistic concurrency
source root confinement and reserved root rejection
route, redirect, link and asset canonicalization
format classifier MIME, signature and extension agreement
Markdown, safe HTML, PDF, Office, presentation, spreadsheet, code, media and archive processor contracts
converter sandbox, timeout, malformed output, macro, decompression bomb, active-content and SSRF policy
rendition identity, Drive storage, reuse, cleanup and rebuild
public page, document viewer, slide viewer, sheet preview, code view, navigation, search, asset and SEO behavior
private, unlisted, quarantine, delete and cross-tenant non-disclosure
event duplicate, ordering, gap, replay and reconciliation
logical source update creates a new immutable Drive version instead of rejecting an existing sources/raw path
Drive input and Knowledgebase output AsyncAPI compatibility
provider-wide generation and per-route public-version isolation
read-through cache freshness and priority revocation
p95 and p99 freshness load evidence
dual-engine database, RLS and migration
standalone and cloud provider parity
Deploy descriptor identity and provider reference compatibility
Web Server unified provider port and error compatibility
complete cross-tenant cache key and event-generation compatibility
no SiteRelease, Deploy Release, Deployment or SiteRevision for ordinary provider lifecycle changes
backup, restore and rebuild
generated SDK and user/admin UI boundaries

governance.implementationClaimGate: status remains proposed and no realtime/publication capability may be claimed until the readiness review P0 findings have executable evidence
humanReviewRequired: approval of the live-mounted model and supersession of the release model
security, privacy, active asset and stale representation policy
processor registry, supported format matrix and conversion SLOs
trusted-active-site isolated origin and supply-chain policy before any uploaded code execution
Deploy and Web Server cross-repository provider contract
freshness SLO and production cache behavior
